purpose: Tray/menu-bar controller. Owns the realtime audio graph, manages plugin loading on a background thread, persists chain order + bypass + plugin state + audio device state through ApplicationProperties, and brokers between the tray menu and the PreferencesWindow / PluginListWindow.
invariants: Destructor order is critical: removeAudioCallback(&player) and stopThread on pluginLoadThread MUST run before deviceManager destructs (deviceManager is declared first in the header, so it destructs LAST). Without manual detach the audio thread keeps firing into player/graph during teardown — guaranteed UAF on shutdown.

pluginLoadGeneration must be incremented before any new loadActivePlugins() or cancelPluginLoading() call so stale callAsync lambdas (onPluginInstanceReady, onAllPluginsLoaded) compare unequal and no-op.
sortedCacheDirty must be set true whenever activePluginList membership or any plugin's 'order' setting changes; getTimeSortedList() trusts the cached vector when !sortedCacheDirty.
Reserved NodeID values kInputNodeId=1_000_000 and kOutputNodeId=1_000_001 must not collide with the per-plugin nodeIds allocated from 'nextPluginNodeId' (which starts at 1 and increments).
gotchas: applyPluginChain uses static_cast<int>(std::time(nullptr)) as the base for 'order' values — overflows in 2038 and two Apply presses within the same second produce identical base values that can collide.

menuInvocationCallback range bounds are written as `id < kDeleteOffset + kEditOffset` (etc.) where kEditOffset doubles as the range size; changing the spacing between offsets in the header breaks every bounds check here.

handleEditPlugin and openPluginEditorFor both silently no-op when graph.getNodeForId returns null — the plugin may still be loading on the background thread. The log line distinguishes 'missing nodeId' from 'node not loaded'.
removePluginsLackingInputOutput operates on knownPluginList (the scanned set), not activePluginList — it runs from PluginListWindow::closeButtonPressed.
ScopedValueSetter<bool> on isHandlingDeviceChange ensures the re-entrancy guard clears even if reconnectGraph throws; a manual flag-set/clear would deadlock device-change events on exception.

PluginLoadThread releases each instance ownership via raw pointer into the callAsync lambda, then re-wraps it in unique_ptr inside the lambda. If IconMenu is destroyed before the lambda fires, SafePointer is null and the unique_ptr deletes the instance — no leak.
reconnectGraph clears all connections via getConnections() + removeConnection per element; AudioProcessorGraph::clear() would also remove nodes which would defeat the purpose.

key_functions.name: loadActivePlugins
key_functions.summary: Full graph rebuild — cancels in-flight load (bumps generation), clears graph, adds I/O nodes, reserves NodeIDs for each active plugin, hands the spec list to PluginLoadThread, wires input→output pass-through so audio flows while plugins load.

key_functions.name: reconnectGraph
key_functions.summary: Rewires the graph from current order+bypass settings without touching nodes. Skips bypassed plugins and plugins whose NodeID has no live node yet (still loading). Falls back to direct input→output when the chain is empty or all-bypassed.

key_functions.name: applyPluginChain
key_functions.summary: Four-step diff applied when Preferences→Apply fires: (1) early-out if identical to current; (2) remove plugins absent from the new chain (close editors, removeNode, clear settings); (3) register newly-added plugins, allocating NodeIDs from nextPluginNodeId; (4) write new sequential 'order' values (baseTime + index) and bypass states, then either loadActivePlugins (if any new plugins were added) or reconnectGraph (reorder/remove-only).

key_functions.name: onPluginInstanceReady
key_functions.summary: Message-thread continuation from PluginLoadThread. Restores state from base64-encoded saved blob, then validates that activePluginList still wants this node before adding it to the graph — guards against the user deleting the plugin mid-load.

key_functions.name: autoMatchSampleRate
key_functions.summary: On device change, snaps the device's sample rate to the closest preferred value (48k → 44.1k → 96k → 88.2k → 192k → 32k) if the current rate isn't supported; falls back to the device's highest rate if none of the preferred ones are available.
